findings.message: The added 'requests' dependency is unpinned (no version constraint), unlike every other entry in this manifest which uses '>=' lower bounds. This breaks the project's pinning convention and makes the dependency graph non-reproducible — a future resolver run could pull in an incompatible major (e.g., a hypothetical requests 3.x) without warning.
findings.suggestion: Add a lower bound consistent with the rest of the manifest, e.g. "requests>=2.32".

findings.message: 'requests' duplicates capability already provided by an existing transitive/dev dependency: httpx>=0.28 is already in the dev group and is the modern, actively-maintained HTTP client (sync + async, HTTP/2, used by FastAPI's TestClient). Adding 'requests' brings a second HTTP stack into the runtime closure (extra supply-chain surface: urllib3, charset-normalizer, idna, certifi) for functionality httpx already covers. requests is in maintenance mode upstream.
findings.suggestion: Drop 'requests' and use httpx for outbound HTTP (promote httpx from the dev group to runtime dependencies if it's needed at runtime). If a specific requests-only API is required, justify it in the PR description.

findings.message: No justification is visible in the diff for adding a new outbound-HTTP dependency. For a service that already wires tenacity + pybreaker (retry/circuit-breaker), introducing a new HTTP client typically needs a paired call site — review whether the consumer code is included and whether it benefits from the existing resilience primitives.
findings.suggestion: Confirm the call site exists, uses tenacity/pybreaker, and that the choice of HTTP client is intentional rather than reflexive.
